description: Controls what the provider management UI may display or mutate. API keys are local-only and never surfaced through the UI. This policy governs read and write boundaries for any provider dashboard, CLI tool, or orchestrator surface that exposes provider pool state.

allowed.notes: Provider identifier is safe to display.
Number of active workers on this provider.
ISO 8601 timestamp when cooldown ends. Null if not in cooldown.
One of: exhaustion, auth, runtime, null.
Hard ceiling on total concurrent workers.
Total workers currently running across all providers.
Count of providers with available capacity.
How the next provider is chosen (e.g. least-loaded).

forbidden.reason: API keys, tokens, and credentials must never be displayed in any UI surface.
Local filesystem paths to credential stores must not be exposed.
Provider responses may contain account identifiers or billing details.
Environment variable secrets must not be read or displayed by the UI.

allowed.notes: Manually disable a provider. Sets status to disabled. Cannot be auto-recovered.

allowed.precondition: Provider must not have an active auth failure.
allowed.notes: Re-enable a manually disabled provider. Blocked if lastFailureClass is auth.
Clear an active cooldown. Provider becomes available immediately.

allowed.validation: Must be a positive integer. Must not exceed globalMaxWorkers.
allowed.notes: Change per-provider concurrency cap. Affects next dispatch only.

allowed.validation: Must be a positive integer. Must be >= sum of all provider maxConcurrency values that are active.
allowed.notes: Change the global worker ceiling. Affects next dispatch only.

forbidden.reason: Secrets must never be written through the UI. Configure locally via env vars, credential manager, or ~/.claude.
Secret source type is a policy decision, not a runtime toggle.
Adding a provider requires updating policy file, state file, and local secret configuration. Not a UI operation.
Removing a provider requires policy file update and orchestrator coordination. Not a UI operation.
Failure patterns are policy-level decisions. UI must not alter classification rules.
Cooldown durations and trigger conditions are policy-level decisions.

rules: API keys are injected from local-only sources (env vars, credential manager, ~/.claude).
The UI reads provider state from .github/ai-state/provider-pool.json, which never contains secrets.
The UI writes mutation commands back to the state file or emits control events — never to the policy file.
Secrets are never logged, cached, or transmitted by the UI layer.
The UI does not proxy API calls to the provider. Workers handle their own API traffic.

auditTrail.notes: All mutations are logged for traceability. Logs never contain secrets.

uiBoundaries.notes: The UI is a state consumer and limited state mutator. It never modifies policy files, source code, or secrets.
